Interface RenderingSurface

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
All Known Subinterfaces:
EmbeddedSurface, HeadlessSurface, WindowSurface
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractGLFWWindowSurface, GL_GLFWWindowSurface
public sealed interface RenderingSurface extends NonThrowingAutoCloseable permits EmbeddedSurface, HeadlessSurface, WindowSurface
Represents a surface where rendering occurs (e.g. window, embedded UI element).

Different implementations provide different capabilities.

Note on acquireBuffer(int, int) and present(Framebuffer): These methods are not intended to be used directly in user code; they were made public solely to allow custom graphics engine implementations. Use RenderCanvas for drawing operations instead.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static enum 
    RenderingSurface.Feature
    Feature flags describing capabilities of this surface.
    static interface 
    RenderingSurface.ResizeListener
    Represents a callback listening to resize events.

    FB_ORIGINAL_SIZE
    Pass this constant to acquireBuffer(int, int) as one of the parameters to request original resolution.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Framebuffer
    acquireBuffer(int prefWidth, int prefHeight)
    Acquires the next available framebuffer for rendering, if any.
    void
    close()
    This method is not expected to throw any checked exceptions.
    default PixelReader
    createPixelReader()
    Returns a pixel reader that can read this surface's contents as ARGB pixels, if RenderingSurface.Feature.READABLE is supported.
    Set<RenderingSurface.Feature>
    getFeatures()
    Returns supported features of this surface.
    int
    getHeight()
    Returns the height of this surface, in pixels.
    default String
    getName()
    Returns the name of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.NAMEABLE is supported.
    default Point2D
    getPos()
    Returns position of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
    default Point2D
    getSize()
    Returns a vector representing dimensions of this surface, in pixels.
    int
    getWidth()
    Returns the width of this surface, in pixels.
    default int
    getX()
    Returns X coordinate of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
    default int
    getY()
    Returns Y coordinate of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
    default void
    hide()
    Hides this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.
    boolean
    isExplicitShouldClose()
    Returns whether a close was explicitly requested with setShouldClose() and clears this flag.
    default boolean
    isFullscreen()
    Checks whether this surface is in fullscreen mode, if RenderingSurface.Feature.FULLSCREENABLE is supported.
    default boolean
    isHidden()
    Checks whether this surface is hidden, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.
    default boolean
    isResizable()
    Checks whether this surface is resizable, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.
    default boolean
    isTranslucent()
    Checks whether this surface is translucent (only the rendered content is visible).
    default void
    onResize(RenderingSurface.ResizeListener callback)
    Adds a resize listener to this surface.
    void
    pollEvents()
    Polls input and system events.
    void
    present(Framebuffer frameBuffer)
    Presents the given frame buffer onto this surface and releases it.
    default void
    setFullscreen(boolean fullscreen)
    Changes the fullscreen state of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.FULLSCREENABLE is supported.
    default void
    setName(String name)
    Sets the name for this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.NAMEABLE is supported.
    default void
    setPos(int newX, int newY)
    Sets the position of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
    default void
    setResizable(boolean resizable)
    Changes the resizable state of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.
    void
    setShouldClose()
    Requests this surface to close; in other words, makes shouldClose() return true on the next call.
    default void
    setSize(int newWidth, int newHeight)
    Sets the new size for this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.
    boolean
    shouldClose()
    Check if this surface should close and clears this flag.
    default void
    show()
    Shows this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.

  • Field Details

    • FB_ORIGINAL_SIZE

      static final int FB_ORIGINAL_SIZE
      Pass this constant to acquireBuffer(int, int) as one of the parameters to request original resolution.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • getWidth

      int getWidth()
      Returns the width of this surface, in pixels.
      Returns:
      the width of this surface

    • getHeight

      int getHeight()
      Returns the height of this surface, in pixels.
      Returns:
      the height of this surface

    • getSize

      default Point2D getSize()
      Returns a vector representing dimensions of this surface, in pixels.
      Returns:
      dimensions of this surface

    • setResizable

      default void setResizable(boolean resizable)
      Changes the resizable state of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported. Surfaces that don't support this feature usually ignore calls to this method.
      Parameters:
      resizable - whether this surface must be resizable

    • isResizable

      default boolean isResizable()
      Checks whether this surface is resizable, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported. Surfaces that don't support this feature must always return false here.
      Returns:
      whether this surface is resizable

    • onResize

      default void onResize(RenderingSurface.ResizeListener callback)
      Adds a resize listener to this surface.

      It may be useful to check the resizable state before adding a callback. Surfaces that are never resizable are free to ignore calls to this method.

      Parameters:
      callback - the listener to call every time this surface gets resized

    • isTranslucent

      default boolean isTranslucent()
      Checks whether this surface is translucent (only the rendered content is visible). This is, in general, determined at creation time and may not change later.
      Returns:
      whether this surface is translucent
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation returns false.

    • pollEvents

      void pollEvents()
      Polls input and system events.

    • acquireBuffer

      Framebuffer acquireBuffer(int prefWidth, int prefHeight)
      Acquires the next available framebuffer for rendering, if any.

      This method is for internal use only; see this note for details.

      Parameters:
      prefWidth - preferred framebuffer width or FB_ORIGINAL_SIZE
      prefHeight - preferred framebuffer height or FB_ORIGINAL_SIZE
      Returns:
      an available render target or null if not available
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is negative (except FB_ORIGINAL_SIZE)

    • present

      void present(Framebuffer frameBuffer)
      Presents the given frame buffer onto this surface and releases it.

      This method is for internal use only; see this note for details.

      Parameters:
      frameBuffer - the frame buffer

    • shouldClose

      boolean shouldClose()
      Check if this surface should close and clears this flag.

      A close may be either:

      • explicit: triggered by setShouldClose()
      • implicit: triggered externally (e.g. window 'X' button or Alt+F4)
      Use isExplicitShouldClose() to distinguish between them.
      Returns:
      whether a close was requested

    • setShouldClose

      void setShouldClose()
      Requests this surface to close; in other words, makes shouldClose() return true on the next call.

    • isExplicitShouldClose

      boolean isExplicitShouldClose()
      Returns whether a close was explicitly requested with setShouldClose() and clears this flag.

      Used by the engine for headful surfaces that might be closed implicitly (e.g. with window 'X' button).

      Returns:
      whether closing has been explicitly requested
      See Also:
      • CloseRequestedEvent
      • shouldClose()

    • getFeatures

      Set<RenderingSurface.Feature> getFeatures()
      Returns supported features of this surface. This can be used to check whether some method will throw UnsupportedOperationException.
      Returns:
      supported features of this surface

    • setSize

      default void setSize(int newWidth, int newHeight)
      Sets the new size for this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.RESIZABLE is supported.
      Parameters:
      newWidth - the new width
      newHeight - the new height
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not resizable
      See Also:
      • getFeatures()

    • getX

      default int getX()
      Returns X coordinate of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      X coordinate of the top-left corner, if supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not movable
      See Also:
      • getFeatures()

    • getY

      default int getY()
      Returns Y coordinate of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      Y coordinate of the top-left corner, if supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not movable
      See Also:
      • getFeatures()

    • getPos

      default Point2D getPos()
      Returns position of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      position of the top-left corner, if supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not movable
      See Also:
      • getFeatures()

    • setPos

      default void setPos(int newX, int newY)
      Sets the position of the top-left corner of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.MOVABLE is supported.
      Parameters:
      newX - the X coordinate of the position
      newY - the Y coordinate of the position
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not movable
      See Also:
      • getFeatures()

    • isHidden

      default boolean isHidden()
      Checks whether this surface is hidden, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      whether this surface is hidden, is supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not hideable

    • show

      default void show()
      Shows this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not hideable

    • hide

      default void hide()
      Hides this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.HIDEABLE is supported.
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not hideable

    • getName

      default String getName()
      Returns the name of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.NAMEABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      the name of this surface, if supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not nameable

    • setName

      default void setName(String name)
      Sets the name for this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.NAMEABLE is supported.
      Parameters:
      name - the new name
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not nameable

    • isFullscreen

      default boolean isFullscreen()
      Checks whether this surface is in fullscreen mode, if RenderingSurface.Feature.FULLSCREENABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      whether this surface is fullscreen, if supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not fullscreenable

    • setFullscreen

      default void setFullscreen(boolean fullscreen)
      Changes the fullscreen state of this surface, if RenderingSurface.Feature.FULLSCREENABLE is supported.
      Parameters:
      fullscreen - whether this surface must be in fullscreen mode
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not fullscreenable

    • createPixelReader

      default PixelReader createPixelReader()
      Returns a pixel reader that can read this surface's contents as ARGB pixels, if RenderingSurface.Feature.READABLE is supported.
      Returns:
      an object that reads this surface by pixels, if supported
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if not readable

    • close

      void close()
      This method is not expected to throw any checked exceptions.

      Note: surfaces must not be closed manually; this is handled by the graphics manager. Use setShouldClose() instead to signal a close request.

      After calling this method, the surface instance should not be used. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
